title: feat: route gemini to Airforce with Vertex fallback
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Routes public `gemini` (gemini-3-flash) to api.airforce, falling back to existing GCP/Vertex on failure
- Same modelId on both targets → billing/pricing unchanged regardless of which serves
- Restores the Portkey `strategy`/`targets` fallback in `portkeyUtils.ts` (revert of `04894e386`, shipped in prod Jan–Jun 2026)
- Drops the `code_execution` default-injection from the gemini transform: Airforce returns a phantom tool_call for it; explicit code_execution requests fall back to Vertex via `on_status_codes` 400
- `on_status_codes` includes 402 (Airforce out of balance) — the real production fallback trigger; failed attempts aren't billed, so falling back is always safe
- Adds `AIRFORCE_API_KEY` to gen secrets (dev/staging/prod) + a unit test for the fallback header shape

Validated end-to-end against the live Portkey gateway: happy path serves Airforce, forced failure falls through to Vertex.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Air traffic control for Gemini
gist.blurb: We've upgraded our Gemini routing! Requests now fly through Airforce by default, with a seamless safety net that catches and redirects to GCP Vertex if there's any turbulence.
gist.summary: Routes public gemini-3-flash requests to api.airforce by default, utilizing a restored Portkey fallback strategy to seamlessly fail over to GCP Vertex on 400 or 402 errors.
gist.impact: Generation reliability is improved through automatic provider failover, while keeping the model ID and billing exactly the same.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as an air traffic controller or pilot, routing glowing data packets between two different server towers (representing Airforce and Vertex).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny pilot's headset, directing glowing blue data packets between two chunky retro server towers. One server has a small cloud icon, the other a tiny jet. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm and nostalgic.
